# `sesam hook install` / `uninstall` only touch git config: they must not load
# the repo (no lock, no audit-log verify, no identity). So they work even with
# no identity available, and fail cleanly on a non-sesam repo.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'
exec sesam init --user admin

# No identity in the environment: a full repo load would fail here, but hook
# install/uninstall must still succeed (they never load the audit log).
env SESAM_ID=
env SESAM_IDENTITY=
exec sesam hook install
exec sesam hook uninstall

# On a git repo that is not a sesam repo, it errors clearly instead of loading.
cd $WORK
mkdir plain
cd plain
exec git init
! exec sesam hook install
stderr 'no sesam repository'
